title: eremophila drummondii
intro: Eremophila drummondii, also known as Drummond's emu bush, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Eremophila drummondii include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. Eremophila drummondii is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its extracts have also been used in topical creams and ointments to support skin health.
